and that's how the body regulates when cells divide, but you can have a situation where it's essentially the equivalent of what you might consider a stuck accelerator.
If you're thinking about an automobile, so if you have a stuck accelerator, and you can only speed up and you can't slow down,
this is analogous to an oncogenic mutation.
Now a different class of gene that's actually the kind of the opposite of an oncogene is called a tumor suppressor,
and tumor suppressors are genes whose normal function is to inhibit growth or even promote death.
